1⃣ Verify & harden the RAG behavior (VERY important)
Before adding features, make sure the system is reliable.
Do this:
● Test easy questions (directly present in text)
● Test paraphrased questions
● Test negative questions (answer not in docs)
Add:
● Confidence threshold (if similarity too low → say “Not found in documents”)
● Max context length (avoid LLM overload)
👉 This prevents hallucinations.
2⃣ Add citations / sources (huge credibility boost)
Right now answers feel “magic”. Make them verifiable.
What to add:
● For each chunk:
○ document name
○ page / section
● Return:
○ answer
○ list of chunks used
Result:
Answer + “Based on Document X, Page Y”
This is how real-world RAG apps work.
3⃣ Improve context assembly (quality jump)
You already assemble context — now refine it.
Enhancements:
● Deduplicate similar chunks
● Order chunks by:
1. similarity
2. document
3. logical flow
● Trim irrelevant lines (headers, footers)
Result: better answers with same LLM.
4⃣ Add conversation memory (optional but powerful)
Turn queries into a chat, not single-shot Q&A.
Approaches:
● Store last N user questions
● Append short conversation summary to prompt
Now users can ask:
“Explain that again”
“What about the second case?”
5⃣ Add evaluation & debugging tools
You need to see what the system is doing.
Add logs for:
● retrieved chunks
● similarity scores
● final context
● LLM prompt
This helps you debug wrong answers fast.
Things to do next-
Add conversation memory
Do it for multiple files

MODULE I:

Definitions of Machine Learning

™* Machine Learningis the science (and art) of programming computers so they
can learn from data.

% Machine Learning is the field of study that gives computers the ability to learn
without being explicitly programmed.

* Acomputer program is said to learn from experience E with respect to some task
T and some performance measure P, if its performance on T, as measured by P,
improves with experience E.

Need to use Machine Learning

Machine Learning is great for:

# Problems for which existing solutions require a lot of hand-tuning or long lists of
rules: one Machine Learning algorithm can often simplify code and p

roblems for which existing solutions require a lot of hand-tuning or long lists of
rules: one Machine Learning algorithm can often simplify code and perform better.

Eg: writing traditional programming vs machine learning approach for a spam filter:

Traditional programming for spam filter detection

Machine Learning approach for spam filter detection

In traditional programming, one writes a detection algorithm for each of the patterns that
you noticed, and the program would flag emails as spam if a number of these patterns are
detected. Since the problem is not trivial, your program will likely become a long list of
complex rules—pretty hard to maintain. In contrast, a spam filter based on

Learning techniques automatically learns which words and phrases are good predictors
of spam by detecting unusually frequent patterns of words in the spam examples
compared to the ham examples. The program is much shorter, easier to maintain, and

Machine Learning shines is for problems that either are too complex for traditional

say you want to start simple and write a program capable of distinguishing the words
“one” and “two.” You might notice that the word “two" starts with a high-pitch sound
("T"), so you could hardcode an algorithm that measures high-pitch sound intensity
and use that to distinguish ones and twos. Obviously this technique will not scale to
thousands of words spoken by millions of very different people in noisy environments

and in dozens of languages. The best solution (at least today) is to write an algorithm
that learns by itself, given many example recordings for each word.

Fluctuating environments: a Machine Learning system can adapt to new data.

However, the path has not been easy. Women in music have faced ongoing
challenges. The industry has historically been male-dominated, especially in
technical areas like music production and sound engineering, where women are
still underrepresented. Many female artists deal with discrimination, unequal
pay, and sexual harassment, which hinder their careers. Stereotypes about the
kind of music women “should” create or how they should present themselves
add more pressure. This can be especially hard for women of color, who face
both racial and gender bias.

but also her cultural significance and strength. Whitney Houston’s powerful
voice and hit songs set new standards for female singers. These artists and
others not only built successful careers but also changed views on what women
in music could achieve. Modern artists carry on this legacy, winning major
awards, breaking records, and leading conversations about gender, race, and
identity.

T and some performance measure P, if its performance on T, as measured by P,
improves with experience E.

Need to use Machine Learning

Machine Learning is great for:

# Problems for which existing solutions require a lot of hand-tuning or long lists of
rules: one Machine Learning algorithm can often simplify code and p

roblems for which existing solutions require a lot of hand-tuning or long lists of
rules: one Machine Learning algorithm can often simplify code and perform better.

Eg: writing traditional programming vs machine learning approach for a spam filter:

Traditional programming for spam filter detection

Machine Learning approach for spam filter detection

In traditional programming, one writes a detection algorithm for each of the patterns that
you noticed, and the program would flag emails as spam if a number of these patterns are
detected. Since the problem is not trivial, your program will likely become a long list of
complex rules—pretty hard to maintain. In contrast, a spam filter based on

the problem is not trivial, your program will likely become a long list of
complex rules—pretty hard to maintain. In contrast, a spam filter based on Machine
Learning techniques automatically learns which words and phrases are good predictors
of spam by detecting unusually frequent patterns of words in the spam examples
compared to the ham examples. The program is much shorter, easier to maintain, and
most likely more accurate.

say you want to start simple and write a program capable of distinguishing the words
“one” and “two.” You might notice that the word “two" starts with a high-pitch sound
("T"), so you could hardcode an algorithm that measures high-pitch sound intensity
and use that to distinguish ones and twos. Obviously this technique will not scale to
thousands of words spoken by millions of very different people in noisy environments

not scale to

and in dozens of languages. The best solution (at least today) is to write an algorithm
that learns by itself, given many example recordings for each word.

Fluctuating environments: a Machine Learning system can adapt to new data.

Ez: In the spam filter detection example, if spammers notice that all their emails

using traditional programming techniques would need to be updated to flag “For U"

writing new rules forever. In contrast,

to be updated to flag “For U"

Introduction
1.1 Project Overview
ChatSphere is a real-time chat web application that allows users to communicate
through direct messaging and group rooms. The application introduces two sepa-
rate chat environments: Work Mode and Private Mode. This enables users to keep
professional conversations and personal conversations separate within the same plat-
form. ChatSphere also includes organizational features such as starred messages with
category grouping and

onversations separate within the same plat-
form. ChatSphere also includes organizational features such as starred messages with
category grouping and file storage with categorization.
1.2 Purpose of the Project
The purpose of ChatSphere is to provide a messaging platform that is structured and
organized. Many chat applications focus mainly on communication but do not offer
efficient ways to manage important messages or store files in a categorized manner.
ChatSphere solves this by introducing message starring, category management, and a
dedicated file storage system.

A white-box testing strategy can either be

Fault-based testing
• A fault-based testing strategy targets to
detect certain types of faults.
• These faults that a test strategy focuses on
constitutes the fault mod el of the strategy.
• An example of a fault-based strategy is
mutation testing.
Coverage-based testing:
• A coverage-based testing strategy attempts to
execute (or cover) certain elements of a
program.
• Popular examples of coverage-based testing
strategies are statement coverage, branch
coverage, multiple condition coverage, and
path coverage-based testing.

• A coverage-based testing strategy typically targets to
execute (i.e., cover) certain program elements for
discovering failures.
• For example, if a testing strategy requires all the
statements of a program to be executed at least once, then
we say that the testing criterion of the strategy is
statement coverage.
• A white-box testing strategy is said to be stronger than
another strategy, if the stronger testing strategy covers all
program elements covered by the weaker testing strategy,
and the stronger strategy additionally covers at least one
program element that is not co

of true/false values.
• So:
• For n = 1 → 2 test cases.
• For n = 2 →4 test cases.
• For n = 3 → 8 test cases.
• For n = 10 → 1024 test cases!
• Hence the number of test cases grows
exponentially with the number of conditions.
• That’s why MC coverage is practical only when n is
small (say, 2–4 conditions); otherwise the test suite
becomes too large.

ChatSphere is a real-time chat web application that allows users to communicate
through direct messaging and group rooms. The application introduces two sepa-
rate chat environments: Work Mode and Private Mode. This enables users to keep
professional conversations and personal conversations separate within the same plat-
form. ChatSphere also includes organizational features such as starred messages with
category grouping and

onversations separate within the same plat-

1.2 Purpose of the Project
The purpose of ChatSphere is to provide a messaging platform that is structured and
organized. Many chat applications focus mainly on communication but do not offer
efficient ways to manage important messages or store files in a categorized manner.
ChatSphere solves this by introducing message starring, category management, and a
dedicated file storage system.
